title: 10 best herbal tinctures for oily skin
intro_desc: Herbal tinctures for oily skin are concentrated liquid extracts of herbs that are used to treat and manage oily skin. They are prepared by soaking the plant material in a solvent such as alcohol or vinegar to extract the active compounds. Some examples of herbal tinctures that are beneficial for oily skin include witch hazel, yarrow, and burdock root. These tinctures help to balance sebum production, reduce inflammation, and tighten pores, resulting in a matte and clear complexion. They can be applied directly to the skin or added to toners and astringents. By using herbal tinctures, individuals with oily skin can experience fewer breakouts, reduced shine, and an overall improvement in the health and appearance of their skin.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yarrow tincture helps with oily skin because it has natural astringent and anti-inflammatory properties. The astringent properties of yarrow help to regulate sebum production, reducing the excess oil on the skin. This makes it an effective natural remedy for oily skin. Additionally, yarrow's anti-inflammatory properties can help soothe irritation and reduce redness, providing relief for those with acne-prone or sensitive skin. Overall, yarrow tincture helps to balance and clarify oily skin, promoting a healthier and more even complexion.
remedy_properties: Astringent: Yarrow tincture's astringent properties help to tighten pores and reduce excess oil production, thanks to its tannin content.
Anti-inflammatory: Yarrow tincture helps soothe irritated skin and reduce redness caused by oily skin, due to the presence of flavonoids and sesquiterpene lactones.
Antibacterial: Yarrow tincture aids in preventing acne and skin infections associated with oily skin, as it contains volatile oils like azulene and camphor.
Antiseborrheic: Yarrow tincture helps control sebum production and regulates the skin's natural oil balance, owing to its salicylic acid and polyacetylenes content.
Wound-healing: Yarrow tincture promotes skin healing and tissue regeneration, which can help with any skin damage or scars caused by oily skin conditions, due to its lactone and sterol constituents.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 ounces of fresh yarrow flowers and leaves, rinse, and let dry for 24 hours.
Chop the dried herbs and place them in a clean glass jar, then add 8 ounces of 80-proof vodka.
Seal the jar and store it in a cool, dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking it daily.
Strain the mixture using cheesecloth, squeezing out the liquid and discarding the plant material.
Store the final tincture in a dark glass bottle, keeping it in a cool, dark place for up to a year.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Burdock tincture helps with oily skin because it has natural anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties that can help regulate sebum production and soothe skin irritations. Burdock is rich in fatty acids, essential minerals, and vitamins, which nourish and balance the skin. It also contains active compounds like arctiin and luteolin that can help reduce excess oil and improve the skin's overall texture and tone. By using burdock tincture as a natural remedy, you can gently cleanse and purify oily skin without stripping it of its natural moisture, promoting a clearer and healthier complexion.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Burdock tincture helps reduce inflammation associated with oily skin, soothing redness and irritation, due to its active constituents like tannins and essential fatty acids.

Astringent: The astringent properties of burdock tincture help tighten pores and control sebum production, reducing the appearance of oily skin, thanks to the tannins and phenolic acids present in the plant.

Antibacterial: Burdock tincture inhibits bacterial growth that can lead to acne and skin infections, making it beneficial for oily skin prone to breakouts, with the help of its antibacterial compounds like arctiin and lignans.

Diuretic: Burdock tincture promotes the elimination of toxins and excess fluids from the body, supporting skin health and reducing oiliness, due to its diuretic constituents such as polyacetylenes and quercetin.

Hormonal balance: Burdock tincture can help regulate hormonal imbalances that contribute to oily skin, as it contains plant steroids and polyacetylenes that may influence sebum production and hormone regulation.
remedy_recipe: Chop 2 ounces of dried burdock root into small pieces and place in a clean glass jar.
Pour 10 ounces of vodka or rum (80-100 proof) over the chopped burdock root, ensuring all pieces are covered.
Seal the jar tightly and store in a cool, dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking the jar gently every few days.
After the designated time, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth into a clean, dark glass bottle, discarding the solid burdock pieces.
Label the bottle with the name, date, and dosage recommendations (typically 20-30 drops in water, 1-3 times daily) and store in a cool, dark place.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wormwood tincture helps with oily skin because it has antiseptic and astringent properties that can help regulate sebum production and reduce the appearance of shiny, greasy skin. The active compound in wormwood, called artemisinin, has been shown to have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help soothe irritated skin and reduce redness. Additionally, wormwood tincture can help tighten pores and improve the overall texture and tone of the skin, giving it a more matte and balanced appearance. By using wormwood tincture as a toner or adding it to your favorite skincare products, you can help keep oily skin under control and promote a clearer, healthier complexion.
remedy_properties: Antiseptic: Wormwood tincture's antiseptic properties help eliminate acne-causing bacteria, which is important for oily skin as it reduces the risk of breakouts. The active constituents responsible for this property are primarily the volatile oils, including thujone and camphor.

Astringent: Wormwood tincture has astringent properties that help shrink and tighten skin pores, preventing excessive oil production and buildup. This is achieved through the tannins and flavonoids present in wormwood.

Anti-inflammatory: Wormwood tincture's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce redness, swelling, and irritation associated with acne and oily skin. The sesquiterpene lactones, such as artabsin and matricin, contribute to this effect.
Regenerative: Wormwood tincture promotes skin cell regeneration, which aids in healing blemishes and scars. This is due to the presence of flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids.

Hormonal balancing: Wormwood tincture helps balance hormones, including androgens that can contribute to excessive sebum production. The active constituents responsible for this property include lactucopicrin and costunolide.
remedy_recipe: Chop 2 ounces of dried wormwood herb and place it in a clean glass jar.
Pour 10 ounces of vodka or grain alcohol (80-100 proof) over the herb, ensuring it is fully submerged.
Seal the jar and store it in a cool, dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking it daily.
Strain the tincture using cheesecloth into a dark glass bottle, discarding the spent herb.
Label the bottle with the name, date, and dosage instructions (e.g., 1-2 droppersful daily) and store in a cool, dark place.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Neem tincture helps with oily skin because it contains anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that can help regulate sebum production, reducing the appearance of oily skin. Neem is also rich in fatty acids and vitamin C, which can help soothe and nourish the skin while balancing its natural oil production. Its astringent properties can help tighten pores and provide a matte finish, leaving the skin looking refreshed and revitalized. Additionally, neem tincture can help reduce redness and inflammation associated with acne, making it an effective natural remedy for oily and blemish-prone skin.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial: Neem tincture's antibacterial properties help control oily skin by reducing the growth of acne-causing bacteria. This is due to the presence of nimbidol and nimbin, which have been shown to exhibit antibacterial activity.

Anti-inflammatory: Neem tincture's anti-inflammatory properties help soothe irritated skin and reduce redness, which is beneficial for oily skin prone to inflammatory acne. This is attributed to the presence of compounds like nimbidin, which has potent anti-inflammatory effects.

Astringent: Neem tincture's astringent properties help tighten pores and reduce excess oil production, giving the skin a matte finish. This is due to the tannins present in neem, which have astringent properties that can help control oiliness.

Sebum-regulating: Neem tincture helps balance sebum production, reducing the appearance of oily skin. This is due to the presence of compounds like gedunin and azadirachtin, which have been shown to regulate sebum production.

Antioxidant: Neem tincture's antioxidant properties help protect the skin from environmental stressors and free radicals, promoting overall skin health. This is due to the presence of compounds like quercetin and kaempferol, which have strong antioxidant properties.
remedy_recipe: Chop fresh neem leaves (50 grams) and place them in a clean jar, filling it up to three-quarters.
Pour 70% alcohol (250 ml) into the jar, ensuring the leaves are fully submerged, then securely close the lid.
Store the jar in a cool, dark place for two to four weeks, shaking it gently every few days.
After the maceration period, strain the mixture using cheesecloth, squeezing the leaves to extract all the liquid.
Pour the resulting neem tincture into a dark glass bottle, label it, and store it in a cool, dry place, away from direct sunlight.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Desert date tincture helps with oily skin because it contains powerful antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties that can help regulate sebum production and soothe inflamed skin. The herbal extracts found in desert date tincture, such as neem and turmeric, have been shown to reduce excess oiliness and improve skin texture, leaving the skin looking clearer and more balanced. Additionally, desert date tincture can help reduce the appearance of pores and provide a natural, gentle exfoliation, making it an excellent addition to any skincare routine for oily skin.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Desert date tincture helps reduce redness and swelling associated with oily skin by inhibiting the release of pro-inflammatory chemicals, thanks to its active constituents like triterpenes and flavonoids.
Astringent: It helps control excessive oil production and shrink enlarged pores by promoting skin contraction, due to the presence of tannins and saponins.
Antibacterial: Desert date tincture helps prevent acne and skin infections caused by bacteria, as it contains active compounds such as lupeol and epicatechin.
Antioxidant: It aids in protecting the skin from oxidative stress and premature aging caused by free radicals, with the help of vitamins A, C, and E, as well as phenolic acids.
Exfoliating: Desert date tincture gently removes dead skin cells and debris, unclogs pores, and improves skin texture, thanks to the presence of alpha-hydroxy acids (AHAs) and natural enzymes.
remedy_recipe: Chop 1 cup of desert date leaves and stems, and place them in a clean glass jar.
Add 2 cups of vodka or pure grain alcohol, ensuring the plant material is fully submerged.
Seal the jar and store it in a cool, dark place for 2-4 weeks, shaking it daily.
Strain the mixture using cheesecloth, squeezing out the liquid and discarding the plant material.
Store the final tincture in a tightly sealed glass container, away from light and heat, for up to a year.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold tincture helps with oily skin because it contains anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties that can help regulate sebum production and soothe irritated skin. The herb's active compounds, including flavonoids and carotenoids, have been shown to reduce inflammation and inhibit the growth of bacteria, making it an effective treatment for acne-prone skin. Additionally, pot marigold tincture has astringent properties that can help tighten pores and improve the skin's overall texture. Its soothing and calming effects can also help reduce redness and irritation, leaving the skin looking clearer and more balanced.
remedy_properties: Antiseptic: Pot marigold tincture has antiseptic properties that help prevent skin infections caused by oily skin, reducing the risk of acne and other skin issues. This is due to the presence of essential oils and alkaloids.

Anti-inflammatory: Its anti-inflammatory properties help soothe irritated skin, reducing redness and inflammation associated with oily skin and acne breakouts, thanks to the flavonoids and carotenoids found in the plant.

Astringent: Pot marigold tincture has astringent properties that help control excess oil production, tighten pores, and provide a matte finish to the skin, attributed to the tannins present in the plant.

Wound healing: The tincture promotes wound healing and tissue regeneration, which is beneficial for oily skin as it aids in the faster recovery of acne lesions and reduces the likelihood of scarring. This is due to the presence of flavonoids, carotenoids, and essential oils.

Antioxidant: Pot marigold tincture has antioxidant properties that help protect the skin from free radical damage, reducing the signs of aging and promoting overall skin health. This is attributed to the high levels of carotenoids and flavonoids found in the plant.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2 oz of dried pot marigold flowers and place them in a clean, dry glass jar.
Pour 10 oz of vodka or rum (80 proof) over the flowers, ensuring they are fully submerged.
Seal the jar tightly and store it in a cool, dark place for two to four weeks, shaking it gently every few days.
After the designated time, strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or fine mesh strainer, discarding the spent flowers.

Transfer the resulting tincture to a clean, dark glass bottle, storing it in a cool, dark place for up to a year. Use a dropper to apply a few drops to a cotton ball and gently blot it onto oily skin as needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: White tea tincture helps with oily skin because it contains high levels of antioxidants and anti-inflammatory properties, which are beneficial for reducing excess oil production and soothing irritated skin. The tincture is made from the leaves and buds of the Camellia sinensis plant, which have been shown to have antibacterial and antifungal properties, making it effective in preventing acne and other skin issues associated with oily skin. Additionally, white tea tincture contains high levels of epigallocatechin gallate (EGCG), a type of catechin that helps to reduce inflammation and improve skin hydration, providing a balanced and healthy complexion. Overall, white tea tincture is a natural and effective solution for managing oily skin.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: White tea tincture's anti-inflammatory properties help soothe irritated skin, which is beneficial for oily skin as it can reduce redness and swelling associated with acne breakouts. The active constituents responsible for this property are catechins, such as EGCG (epigallocatechin gallate).

Antioxidant property: White tea tincture contains high levels of antioxidants that help protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to excess oil production and clogged pores. The primary antioxidants in white tea are polyphenols, including EGCG.

Antimicrobial property: White tea tincture has antimicrobial properties that can help eliminate the bacteria that cause acne, making it an effective remedy for oily skin prone to breakouts. The major antimicrobial compounds in white tea are tannins and flavonoids.

Astringent property: White tea tincture has a mild astringent effect, which helps to tighten pores and reduce the appearance of excess oil on the skin. The astringent properties are attributed to the tannins present in white tea.

Sebum regulation property: White tea tincture can help regulate sebum production, reducing the amount of oil on the skin's surface. This property is due to the presence of catechins and other polyphenols, which have been shown to inhibit 5-alpha-reductase, an enzyme involved in sebum production.
remedy_recipe: Chop 1 cup of dried organic white tea leaves and 1/2 cup of dried organic herbs known to benefit oily skin, such as lavender and yarrow, into small pieces.
Combine the chopped herbs and tea leaves with 2 cups of vodka or grain alcohol in a clean glass jar, ensuring the herbs are fully submerged.
Seal the jar and store it in a cool, dark place for 2-4 weeks, shaking the mixture daily.
After the designated time, strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a clean glass jar or bottle, discarding the spent herbs.
Label the jar or bottle with the contents, date, and recommended dosage (usually 1-2 droppersful up to three times a day), and store in a cool, dark place.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Cannabis tincture helps with oily skin because it contains cannabidiol (CBD), a compound that has been shown to have sebostatic and anti-inflammatory effects. Sebostatic agents help reduce the production of sebum, the oily substance secreted by the skin's sebaceous glands, which can lead to clogged pores and acne. CBD's anti-inflammatory properties can also help soothe irritated skin and reduce redness. Additionally, cannabis tincture can help regulate the skin's oil production, leaving it looking balanced and matte. Its potent antioxidant properties can also help protect the skin from environmental stressors, promoting a clearer and healthier complexion.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Cannabis tincture can reduce inflammation, which is important for oily skin as it can help decrease the production of sebum and alleviate skin irritation. This property is attributed to cannabinoids such as cannabidiol (CBD) and tetrahydrocannabinol (THC).

Sebum regulation: Cannabis tincture can help regulate sebum production, which is crucial for oily skin as excessive sebum can lead to acne and other skin issues. The terpene beta-caryophyllene and cannabinoids like CBD contribute to this property.

Antibacterial: Cannabis tincture has antibacterial properties, which are essential for oily skin as they can help prevent the growth of acne-causing bacteria, thereby reducing the risk of breakouts. The terpene limonene and cannabinoids like CBD possess antibacterial properties.

Antioxidant: Cannabis tincture contains antioxidants that can protect the skin from oxidative stress and damage, which is important for oily skin as it can help reduce signs of aging and improve overall skin health. The cannabinoids CBD and THC, as well as flavonoids, contribute to these antioxidant effects.

Wound healing: Cannabis tincture can promote wound healing, which is beneficial for oily skin as it can help treat and prevent acne lesions. This property is attributed to cannabinoids like CBD and THC, which have been shown to have wound-healing and tissue-repair properties.
remedy_recipe: Chop 1 ounce of cannabis flower and 1 cup of calendula flowers, then combine in a large jar.
Pour 2 cups of high-proof alcohol (such as vodka) over the herbs, ensuring they are fully submerged.
Seal the jar and store in a cool, dark place for 4-6 weeks, shaking daily.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth into a clean jar, squeezing out as much liquid as possible.
Discard the spent herbs and store the tincture in a cool, dark place for up to a year.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Gotu kola tincture helps with oily skin because it has astringent and anti-inflammatory properties that can help regulate sebum production and soothe irritated skin. This herb is known to improve circulation, which can help unclog pores and promote healthy skin cell growth. Additionally, gotu kola contains compounds that can help reduce the appearance of enlarged pores and minimize the production of excess oil, leaving the skin looking more balanced and matte. Its soothing and calming effects can also help reduce redness and inflammation, providing relief for those who experience acne or other skin irritations.
remedy_properties: Sebum regulation: Gotu kola tincture helps regulate sebum production, reducing excess oil on the skin. This is important for oily skin as excessive sebum production can lead to acne and other skin issues. The active constituents responsible for this property include triterpenoids and saponins.

Anti-inflammatory: Gotu kola tincture has anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe irritated skin. This is crucial for oily skin as inflammation can worsen skin conditions and lead to breakouts. The main active constituents responsible for this property are asiaticoside, madecassoside, and madasiatic acid.

Antimicrobial: Gotu kola tincture has antimicrobial properties that help prevent the growth of bacteria and fungi on the skin. This is important for oily skin as it can be more prone to bacterial and fungal infections, leading to acne and other skin issues. The active constituents responsible for this property include tannins, flavonoids, and essential oils.

Wound healing: Gotu kola tincture promotes wound healing and tissue regeneration. This is important for oily skin as it can help reduce the appearance of acne scars and other skin blemishes. The active constituents responsible for this property include asiaticoside, madecassoside, and madasiatic acid.

Antioxidant: Gotu kola tincture has antioxidant properties that help protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals. This is important for oily skin as it can help prevent premature aging and improve overall skin health. The active constituents responsible for this property include flavonoids, tannins, and essential oils.
remedy_recipe: Chop 2 ounces of dried gotu kola leaves and stems into small pieces, then place them in a clean glass jar.
Pour 1 pint of vodka or grain alcohol (80-100 proof) over the herbs until fully submerged, then tightly seal the jar.
Store the jar in a cool, dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking it daily to ensure even extraction.
After the desired steep time, strain the mixture using cheesecloth or a fine mesh strainer, pressing out any excess liquid from the herbs.
Transfer the resulting tincture to a clean, amber glass dropper bottle, label it with the ingredients and date, and store in a cool, dark place for up to a year.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: German chamomile tincture helps with oily skin because it has anti-inflammatory and healing properties that can soothe and balance the skin. It contains active compounds like alpha-bisabolol and azulene, which have been shown to reduce inflammation, redness, and irritation, making it an ideal natural remedy for oily and acne-prone skin. German chamomile tincture also has astringent properties that can help regulate sebum production, reducing the appearance of shiny and greasy skin. Its soothing and calming effects can also help alleviate stress-related skin issues, promoting a clearer and more even complexion.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: German chamomile tincture's anti-inflammatory properties, due to active constituents like alpha-bisabolol and matricin, help soothe irritated skin and reduce redness, which are common issues with oily skin.
Astringent: The astringent properties of German chamomile tincture, attributed to its tannin content, help control excess oil production and tighten pores, providing a matte finish for oily skin.
Antibacterial: The antibacterial properties of German chamomile tincture, owing to its chamazulene content, help prevent acne and skin infections that can be associated with oily skin.

Antioxidant: German chamomile tincture's antioxidant properties, derived from its high levels of flavonoids and terpenoids, protect oily skin from environmental stressors and help maintain skin health.

Soothing: The soothing properties of German chamomile tincture, due to its azulene content, help alleviate skin irritations, reduce itchiness, and promote overall skin comfort for individuals with oily skin.
remedy_recipe: Chop 1 cup of dried German chamomile flowers and place them in a clean glass jar.
Pour 2 cups of vodka or rum (80-100 proof) over the chopped flowers, ensuring they are fully submerged.
Seal the jar and store it in a cool, dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking it daily.
Strain the mixture using a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh strainer, discarding the solids and keeping the liquid.
Transfer the liquid to a dark glass bottle, storing it in a cool, dark place for up to a year. Use a few drops on oily skin as needed.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat oily skin with herbal tinctures is by using natural astringents like witch hazel or yarrow. These herbs help to balance sebum production, tighten pores, and reduce shine. Combine equal parts of the chosen tincture with water and apply it to your face using a cotton ball, avoiding the eye area. For best results, use it twice daily after cleansing and before moisturizing. Always perform a patch test first to ensure you're not sensitive to the tincture.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to oily skin that are treated with herbal tinctures include acne, blackheads, and skin inflammation. Tinctures made from herbs such as burdock, calendula, and dandelion are known to regulate sebum production, reduce inflammation, and fight bacteria, making them effective in treating these skin conditions. Burdock tincture, for instance, has been used for centuries to purify the blood and promote healthy skin, while calendula tincture is known for its anti-inflammatory and wound-healing properties. Dandelion tincture, on the other hand, helps to detoxify the liver, which can improve overall skin health. These herbal tinctures offer a natural and holistic approach to managing oily skin and its associated ailments.
